dialogues: I used to be an adventurer like you...
Welcome, stranger.
Need something?
Have you seen my chicken?
I lost my socks again.
There are monsters nearby.
Don't go into the forest at night.
I wouldn't go that way if I were you.
The mayor is waiting for you.
I'm just a simple merchant. Mostly.
You look like you've seen some things.
I don't talk to strangers. But here we are.
They say the dungeon holds great treasure. They also say it's cursed.
Business is... business.
My back is killing me.
Have you completed your quest yet?
The hero never comes when you need them.
Watch your coin purse around here.
Something wicked this way comes.
You're the one they call the Chosen One? Hm.
I sell things. Sometimes. If I feel like it.
Ask me again after the event.
The king is busy. As always.
Don't anger the blacksmith.
These are dark times. Or it might just be the weather.
Have you tried talking to the innkeeper?
I've seen a lot of adventurers. Most don't come back.
My grandmother warned me about people like you.
I just work here.
You seem trustworthy enough. Probably.
The prophecy was unclear on this one.
This isn't what I studied for.
You didn't hear this from me.
I'm not involved.
Don't touch my cart.
Have you slain the dragon yet?
The guild has a quest for someone like you.
I used to be important. Now I sell fish.
Lovely weather if you ignore the ominous clouds.
You're either very brave or very lost.
The last adventurer who came through here... well.
I've been standing here for three days.
Refresh the page and try again.
My inventory is limited. Like my patience.
The king's taxes are outrageous, frankly.
There are herbs in the forest if you need healing. I'm just saying.
The wizard hasn't left his tower in weeks.
I don't ask questions. I just restock the shelves.
You look like trouble.
Keep moving, stranger.
The dungeon is sealed. By law. Technically.
I have exactly what you need. It's $9,999.
Buy something or move along.
The quest board is that way. I think.
We don't talk about the old wizard.
Please don't break anything.
I'm not a quest giver. I'm just waiting for a friend.
Rumours say the swamp is enchanted.
I've been patrolling this hallway for forty years.
Nothing to see here.
I am programmed to say three things and I've said two.
Danger? In this economy?
Everything here is definitely not cursed.
I am perfectly happy in this village. Everything is fine.
The blacksmith broke the forge again.
I once had a dream I could leave. Wild.
You're blocking my respawn point.
This town has a lore. I'd explain but it's long.
I'm not giving you a side quest. I'm busy.
They say the old ruins hold power. Or rats. One of those.
The festival is next week. Everything is chaos.
I lost my key three quests ago.
The guards are corrupt. But they're OUR corrupt guards.
This village has a hero problem. Too many, not enough.
I only sell on Tuesdays.
My wares are of the highest quality. Most of them.
Something is not right in the eastern woods.
I would help but I have a condition. It's called Tuesday.
If you find any gold, I'm right here.
The map is wrong but it's the only one we have.
You'll need my help eventually. They always do.
I've been here since the loading screen.
Every hero needs a weird NPC encounter. This is yours.
I have 300 pieces of useless dialogue and this is one of them.
Talk to me again for more repetitive responses.
You were supposed to find me in Act 2. You're early.
I know where the hidden chest is. But I'm not telling.
The final boss is real. I've met them. Nice person, actually.
You can't fast travel from here. Sorry.
I'm marked on the map as optional. I know.
The 100% completion achievement requires talking to me.
I drop a rare item if you complete my eight-part quest.
Come back when you're stronger.
My shop has a 30-day return policy.
Beware the dungeon on level three. That's where the real danger is.
The secret area is behind the waterfall. You didn't hear it from me.
I once saved the world. Nobody added it to the lore.
I have a quest but you look busy.
Talk to me three more times for a meaningful response.
I respawn in this spot every morning. It's fine.
Explore everything. Some of it is worth finding.
I'm the last NPC in the village. Make of that what you will.
Everything is proceeding normally here.
I can't leave this building. But theoretically, yes.
I've been expecting you. Since last Tuesday.
The adventure begins here. Probably.
Wander carefully. Some areas are loading.
I'm an ambience character. I just say things.
The ending of this game will surprise you.
I gave the main character advice in the prologue. They're doing fine.
Tread carefully. There are things in the dark.
Buy my wares or perish. Respectfully.
This game world has 14,000 unique lines. Glad you found mine.
The plot thickens around here.
I have a backstory but no cutscene budget.
If you find the missing pages, bring them to me.
Rumours of dark magic in the east have kept me busy.
I used to patrol the courtyard. Budget cuts.
My name is listed in the credits. Small font.
The dragon is a metaphor. I think.
Not everyone needs a quest to have purpose.
Tell the king I said hello. He won't remember me.
I'm just grateful to be included.
My dialogue tree is very shallow but I appreciate the interaction.
The hero will save us. Probably. Eventually.
The lore is deep here. I don't understand most of it.
I've been modded into another game before. Weird experience.
I don't get a character arc but I try.
Keep your head down and your inventory organised.
Trust no one. But also trust someone. It's complicated.
I'm the only fully voiced NPC in this region. Enjoy it.
I exist on a 2-second loop and this is day 4,000.
My internal clock says it's always noon.
This isn't my final form.
I have feelings. The developers confirmed it. In a patch note.
Someday I'll be a main character. For now, atmosphere.
My role is ambient. My heart is not.
I remember the tutorial. You were different then.
They said you'd come. The stars agreed. The server didn't.
If you're reading this, you're already in too deep.
This is a big world. You're doing fine.
Choose your next words carefully. I have three responses.
Everything leads somewhere. Even this conversation.
I'll be here if you need me. I'm always here.
Safe travels. Whatever that means here.
You're the protagonist of your own story. I'm the extended universe.
I exist between your quests. It's peaceful.
I hope your journey is worth it.
Farewell, traveller. Come back never. Or do. I'll be here.
Some of the best lore is in the optional conversations. Like this one.
locations: A farm that's seen some things
An inn at the edge of the world
A tavern at the end of the universe
The final floor of an unfinished dungeon
A 24-hour noodle shop at the crossroads
A city that only exists at night
A merchant quarter with no customers
A hidden valley with no enemies
A landmark that's just a rock
A place you have to return to for a collectible
An island you need a boat for
A location cut from the main story
A landmark with a sad backstory
A hidden shop in a waterfall
A place described as dangerous but isn't
The legendary city that turned out to be fine
A level 1 area everyone revisits for lore
A biome that exists only in the corner of the map
An extremely average town with dark secrets
A tavern run by someone with a huge quest
A building you enter in Act 1 and never revisit
The place where the tutorial NPC went after
A secret floor accessed by pressing a specific wall
An area that loads slightly slower than the others
A zone that's technically DLC but feels base game
An unmarked grave location with ambient music
A field that transitions into everything else
The last safe place before the final act
A city with the best in-game soundtrack
An inn that only appears at certain times
A place where the NPCs say ominous things for no reason
A starting area that becomes meaningful later
A scenic overlook with a codex entry
rarities: We Made a New Tier for This
So Rare It Loops Back to Common
The Devs Didn't Account for This
